# SDG Target Mapping {#sec-sdg-target-mapping}

::: {.chapterabstract}
Draft abstract: this chapter closes Part I by mapping technical
capability clusters from Chapter 2 onto specific SDG indicators, and by
returning to the institutional comparison opened in
@sec-institutional-baseline.
:::

::: {.keywords}
SDG alignment taxonomy &middot; indicator mapping &middot; knowledge-policy nexus &middot; data provenance
:::

## 3.1 Alignment Taxonomy
- Mapping technical capabilities to specific SDG indicators
  (e.g., Target 9.4, 12.4) rather than headline SDG numbers alone.

## 3.2 Identifying Negative Externalities and Trade-offs
- Where "AI for good" claims and material/energy costs diverge.

## 3.3 Comparative Knowledge Mapping: UNU, OECD, ADB
- Extends @sec-institutional-baseline into a knowledge-to-policy
  comparison across the three institutions.
- Framed as fostering open, collaborative innovation across
  multilateral agencies, not a ranking exercise.

## 3.4 Data Governance and Provenance for SDG Claims
- Minimum provenance metadata required before a claim can be cited in
  downstream chapters (DPP in @sec-digital-product-passports; RegTech in
  @sec-regtech-defa-alignment).

::: {.callout-tip title="Bridge to Part II"}
Part II turns this evidence base into the design layer: the extended ITU
canvases.
:::
